intro:
Jack'd is a dating and social networking app primarily for gay, bi, trans, and queer men of color. It has a loyal community but suffers from documented security issues and a limited free tier.

Embir offers an alternative with verified profiles, strict bidirectional orientation filtering, and reporting and blocking — core connection features are free.

comparisonTable.embir: Full features core connection features are free
Full trans identity support in matching

sections.title: Security and verification — where Jack'd struggles
sections.content: Jack'd has faced documented security issues including data exposure incidents. Embir's approach is fundamentally different: selfie verification required at signup, reporting and blocking tools, and strict bidirectional filtering that prevents orientation mismatches.
highlights: Selfie verification — barrier to fake accounts
reporting and blocking — every report reviewed

verdict: Jack'd serves its niche community but suffers from security vulnerabilities. Embir offers a safer alternative with verified profiles, reporting and blocking, full trans/NB inclusivity, and free access at launch.
ctaText: Safer LGBTQ+ dating, verified — join Embir

faq.a: Embir has selfie verification at signup, reporting and blocking tools, and documented zero-tolerance policies for harassment. Significantly more secure by design.

faq.q: Is Embir inclusive for men of color?
faq.a: Yes. Embir is a universal platform where all backgrounds, orientations, and identities are full participants.
Yes. Core connection features are free for everyone.
